commit.message:
feat(chatview): harden pi chat and no-focus desktop automation

Summary:
- extract chat model selection into a dedicated controller and
  centralize setup prompt helpers for cleaner chatview ownership
- preserve non-Pi transcript history across Pi sync, reset fresh-chat
  composer state correctly, and clean up duplicate embeddings status
  bars after reloads
- harden the desktop automation bridge, stable-client bootstrap,
  reload/chatview stress runners, and release preflight privacy and
  gitignore safety checks

Rationale:
- keep the Pi-integrated chatview simpler while fixing the real model
  switch, fresh-chat, and transcript continuity regressions exposed by
  live vault testing
- make no-focus Obsidian automation the canonical release-proof path,
  including reload churn, bridge recovery, and duplicate-status-bar
  detection
- block local-only, secret-like, or missing-ignore release hazards
  before version tagging and release publication

Tests:
- git diff --check
- git check-ignore -v .env.local .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-debug-20260224-015345.log .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-debug-20260224-015444.log .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-verbose-20260224-015427.log

- npm run build
- npm test

- node scripts/reload-local-obsidian-plugin.mjs

- npm run test:native:desktop:stress

commit.message:
COE-312: Add session notes, outcome states, and artifact registry (#15)

* feat(session,artifact): add session notes, outcome states, and artifact registry

Summary:
- Add SessionOutcomeState enum (valid, invalid, aborted) to domain models
- Add notes and outcome_state fields to Session domain model and ORM
- Add experiment_id field to Artifact model for experiment-scoped artifacts
- Create database migration 520517cac40b for schema changes
- Update SessionService with outcome state handling and notes support
- Create ArtifactRegistryService for artifact management
- Add ArtifactRepository interface for artifact persistence
- Update session CLI with --notes option on create, --outcome on finalize
- Add session add-notes CLI command for updating notes
- Create artifact CLI commands: register, list, show, remove
- Link artifacts to sessions or experiments (at least one required)

Rationale:
- Operators need to document session context with notes during/after runs
- Outcome states allow marking sessions as invalid/aborted while keeping them
  visible for audit (they can be excluded from comparisons)
- Artifact registry enables attaching exports/reports to sessions or experiments
  for traceability and comparison workflows

Tests:
- make test: 77 tests passed
- make type-check: passed

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: address review feedback - remove duplicate SessionService, add comprehensive tests

- Remove duplicate SessionService from services_abc.py (keep services/session_service.py)
- Add tests for add-notes CLI command (3 tests)
- Add tests for artifact CLI commands (6 tests)
- Add tests for SQLArtifactRepository (7 tests)
- Add tests for update_session_notes service method (4 tests)
- Fix UUID slicing in artifact list command
- Add ArtifactRegistryService to exports

All 235 tests passing

* fix: remove ArtifactRegistryService due to architectural mismatch

- Removed ArtifactRegistryService from services_abc.py
- The service used domain models but repository expects ORM models
- This is a broader architectural pattern in the codebase
- CLI already uses SQLArtifactRepository directly with proper validation
- Updated exports in services/__init__.py

235 tests passing

* COE-312: Address PR feedback - restore --status parameter, add evidence

- Restore --status parameter to finalize command for backward compatibility
- Add --outcome parameter as optional (defaults to 'valid')
- Add test for --status parameter backward compatibility
- Create EVIDENCE_COE-312.md with CLI examples for all new features
- Fix linting issues with ruff --fix
- All 236 tests passing

* COE-312: Remove developer workflow scripts

- Remove scripts/create_workpad.py (Linear workflow helper)
- Remove scripts/linear_helper.py (Linear API helper)
- These were development tools not part of the benchmark system

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(mcp): tighten placeholder-backed auth headers

Summary:
- require sensitive MCP header values to be fully placeholder-backed instead of accepting any placeholder substring
- inspect env-expanded header values for vault placeholders so missing vault keys fail preflight with actionable auth_required details
- add regression tests for mixed raw-plus-placeholder headers and env-backed vault placeholders, and record the review follow-up in the trust-boundary docs

Rationale:
- PR review found that mixed raw secrets could bypass the new MCP credential-isolation boundary by appending a dummy placeholder
- env-backed vault placeholders were not being re-scanned after env substitution, which could degrade missing credentials into opaque downstream auth failures

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/mcp.py backend/src/tools/mcp_manager.py backend/tests/test_mcp_api.py backend/tests/test_mcp_manager.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_mcp_api.py tests/test_mcp_manager.py tests/test_tools_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_main_lists_available_scenarios -q
- git diff --check
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(identity): address review feedback after rebase

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(virtual-camera): stabilize demand lifecycle and release flow

Summary:
- gate host preview on real capture demand so the menubar app stays idle
  without an open window or external virtual camera consumer
- move early wake-up to authorizedToStartStream, add demand inspection and
  Chromium/native probe harnesses, and harden the background test scripts
- add local install and DMG publishing skills, bump the app and sysext to
  0.1.0 (10), and refresh the manual release asset workflow

Rationale:
- the host was keeping the physical camera alive after UI teardown because
  preview startup and shutdown were still coupled to appearance state
- the sysext wake-up path was too noisy and a stale extension process hid
  the real behavior until the bundle version forced a clean restart
- reproducible local probes and a repeatable DMG flow are needed so the
  fix can be validated and shipped without manual QA loops

Tests:
- xcodebuild -project Reframe.xcodeproj -scheme Reframe -destination
  'platform=macOS' -derivedDataPath build/test test
- ./.agents/skills/local-test/scripts/build_and_install.sh
- build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe print-demand
- CLI_PATH="$PWD/build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe" APP_PATH='/Applications/Reframe.app' ./Scripts/run-chromium-background-virtual-camera-probe.sh
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/build_release_dmg.sh /Applications/Reframe.app build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/publish_release_asset.sh v0.1.0 build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor: separate file stream aborts from fusillade errors (#183)

## Summary
- add `FileStreamItem::Abort` and `FileStreamResult` so
producer-initiated aborts are no longer surfaced as fusillade validation
errors
- update `create_file_stream` to return `Result<FileStreamResult>` and
roll back cleanly on abort
- deprecate `FileStreamItem::Error(String)` while preserving the old
path for compatibility, and update tests to cover abort behavior

## Why
Producer-side aborts are not owned by fusillade. Treating them as
`Err(FusilladeError::ValidationError(String))` forced callers to
round-trip their own failures through fusillade, erased type
information, and encouraged fragile string matching. This change
separates producer intent (`Abort`) from actual fusillade failures and
makes the ownership boundary explicit.

## Context
Fixes
https://linear.app/doubleword/issue/COR-14/clean-abort-signaling-in-fusillade

## Local verification
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check --lib`
- `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings`

I also attempted to run the DB-backed CI path locally, including
starting PostgreSQL with the system binaries and Debian cluster tooling,
but this sandbox blocks the required privilege transition to the
`postgres` user, so full local `cargo test` / `just ci` could not be
completed here.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: Batched LLM request processing daemon with efficient request coalescing and per-model concurrency control

commit.message:
fix: improve context overflow detection and condenser handling (#63)

* fix(openhands): improve context overflow detection and condenser handling

Summary:
- Extract error details from conversation state for better error detection
- Reset conversations when workflow now has condenser but existing doesn't
- Start local server for CLI rehydrate command when needed

Rationale:
- Error detection was only capturing "error" status, not actual error message
- Old conversations without condenser should be reset to get condenser benefits
- CLI rehydrate command now works without a pre-running server

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands --package opensymphony-cli (all pass)
- cargo clippy --package opensymphony-openhands --package opensymphony-cli (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(openhands): include conversation stats in state mirror for token tracking

Summary:
- Merge conversation.stats into raw_state when applying conversation to state mirror
- This fixes token tracking showing 0 because stats.usage_to_metrics.default.accumulated_token_usage was not available

Rationale:
- The accumulated_token_usage() function reads from stats.usage_to_metrics.default.accumulated_token_usage
- OpenHands provides this data in the Conversation.stats field from the REST API
- Without merging stats into raw_state, token tracking would always return None

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands (all pass)
- Added state_mirror_includes_stats_from_conversation test

commit.message:
fix: enable condenser by default and auto-recover from context overflow (#62)

* fix: enable condenser by default and auto-recover from context overflow

The OpenHands built-in condensation mechanism was not enabled by default,
allowing conversations to exceed the model's maximum context length
(262,143 tokens). This caused ConversationRunError failures that required
manual rehydration.

Changes:
- Enable LLMSummarizingCondenser by default (max_size=240, keep_first=2)
  when the condenser block is omitted from WORKFLOW.md
- Add context overflow error detection (prompt-too-long patterns)
- Auto-recover from context overflow: close old stream, rehydrate to fresh
  conversation with token preservation, retry the turn
- Fix rehydrate_conversation() to return RehydrationFailed with actual
  cause instead of opaque UnexpectedEarlyResult
- Update tests and documentation for new condenser default

* refactor(openhands): extract turn execution and add retry guard

Summary:
- Extract turn execution into execute_turn() helper method
- Extract recovery logic into recover_from_context_overflow() helper
- Add MAX_CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_RETRIES=1 guard to prevent infinite loops
- Reduce nesting from 4+ levels to 2 levels in run_with_observer()

Rationale:
- Addresses PR review feedback on code complexity and infinite retry risk
- Helper methods eliminate code duplication of turn execution flow
- Retry guard ensures at most one recovery attempt per session

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands (85 passed)
- cargo clippy --package opensymphony-openhands (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: 削除モーダルを制御前提に揃える

DeletePaymentModal の非制御利用前提を削除し、open と onClose を必須にした。\nStory も制御前提に揃えてレビュー指摘に対応した。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: MSW の削除レスポンスを簡素化する

payments の MSW ハンドラを成功レスポンス中心の実装に寄せた。\n削除失敗 Story の loader では handler を reset してから override するようにした。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(evals): Stabilize workflow behavior and harness auth (#127)

Stabilize the eval workflow paths that were still failing or proving
brittle. OAuth callback resume now reads persisted thread state
directly, subscribed follow-up routing has clearer guidance for genuine
Junior follow-ups, and the GitHub skill now keeps duplicate checks
silent in the final reply.

This also hardens the harness itself. Vitest env loading now preserves
caller-provided env vars while still letting later .env files override
earlier ones, and eval log capture is scoped to the `slackEval` task so
timed-out scenarios cannot leak gateway-auth failures into later evals.

The branch also adds `pnpm cloudflare:token`, which regenerates the
named Cloudflare tunnel token with `cloudflared` and upserts it into the
root `.env.local`. That makes the stable local Slack tunnel workflow
repeatable after `vercel env pull` rewrites `.env.local`.

Added route-level integration coverage for generic OAuth and MCP OAuth
resume, plus focused unit coverage for env precedence and the eval
harness behavior seam. Locally this passes the targeted OAuth resume
suites, subscribed-message routing suites, env loader tests, eval
harness unit tests, `pnpm --filter @sentry/junior exec tsc --noEmit`,
and a real `pnpm cloudflare:token` refresh for the default `junior-dev`
tunnel. The remaining GitHub skill eval should be rerun outside Codex
against this branch.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

  runners/docs for no-focus validation against an already-open vault

  bootstrap can heal itself without renderer automation

- model switching now needs a contained, cleaner surface that does not
  leak off-screen or spread selection logic across the chat view

  path keeps live chat/model verification available in the background
- canonical scripts/docs needed to point at the new desktop runner so
  the validated workflow stays reproducible

- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand --passWithNoTests

  private-vault (model switching, chat send, file read/write, web
  fetch, YouTube transcript)

fix(evals): align guardian learning runtime contracts

Summary:
- update the Batch D eval harness to assert queued and deferred delivery outcomes where evidence-weighted suppression now wins over direct delivery
- refresh runtime eval expectations so generic feedback no longer implies direct or native-channel learning and procedural adaptation expects eight active procedural memories
- record the CI follow-up root cause in the guardian intelligence implementation notes

Rationale:
- backend CI was failing because the eval harness still encoded the pre-Batch D policy contract after delivery arbitration and suppression learning changed runtime behavior
- the shipped code was behaving correctly; the stale assumptions were in harness event lookups and test assertions

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- .venv/bin/python -c 'import ast, asyncio, pathlib; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; source=pathlib.Path(tests/test_eval_harness.py).read_text(); mod=ast.parse(source); fn=next(node for node in mod.body if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name==test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details); call=next(n for n in ast.walk(fn) if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and getattr(n.func, id, ) == run_runtime_evals); names=[elt.value for elt in call.args[0].elts]; summary=asyncio.run(run_runtime_evals(names)); print({failed: summary.failed, errors: [{name: r.name, error: r.error} for r in summary.results if not r.passed]})'
- .venv/bin/python -c 'import ast, asyncio, pathlib; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; source=pathlib.Path(tests/test_eval_harness.py).read_text(); mod=ast.parse(source); fn=next(node for node in mod.body if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name==test_memory_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details); call=next(n for n in ast.walk(fn) if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and getattr(n.func, id, ) == run_runtime_evals); names=[elt.value for elt in call.args[0].elts]; summary=asyncio.run(run_runtime_evals(names)); details={r.name: r.details for r in summary.results}; print({failed: summary.failed, errors: [{name: r.name, error: r.error} for r in summary.results if not r.passed], active_procedural_memory_count: details[procedural_memory_adaptation_behavior][active_procedural_memory_count]})'
- .venv/bin/python -c 'import asyncio; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; summary=asyncio.run(run_runtime_evals()); print({failed: summary.failed, errors: [{name: r.name, error: r.error} for r in summary.results if not r.passed]})'

commit.message:
docs: add per-crate readmes (#40)

## Summary
- add dedicated `README.md` files for each workspace crate with
crate-specific descriptions, docs links, badges, feature notes, and
licensing
- wire each crate README into crate-level rustdocs via `#![doc =
include_str!("../README.md")]` so GitHub and generated API docs stay
aligned
- add `description` and `readme` package metadata for every crate, and
include the new README in the `cargo-airbender` package manifest
- update crate dependency snippets to use Git dependencies on the `main`
branch and add an alpha-state warning banner while crates are
unpublished

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --locked` with
`ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo check -p airbender-sdk -p
airbender-host -p cargo-airbender`

## Notes
- plain workspace doc or host-side verification currently needs
`ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true` in this repo because the existing
CUDA-dependent toolchain path cannot determine a local CUDA Toolkit
version

---------

commit.message:
fix(guardian): backfill upgrade column and sync success learning

Summary:
- add legacy SQLite backfill and index creation for guardian_interventions.active_project
- refresh procedural learning memories when outcome transitions touch success or failure evidence states
- add regressions for upgraded DBs and delivery-only success synchronization, and record the PR follow-up in implementation docs

Rationale:
- upgraded local databases created before active_project existed would fail ORM reads and writes at runtime
- successful delivered or feedback_received transitions now affect weighted evidence, so durable scoped guidance must refresh on those transitions as well

Tests:

- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/db/engine.py backend/src/guardian/feedback.py backend/tests/test_db_engine.py backend/tests/test_guardian_feedback.py backend/tests/test_learning_arbitration.py backend/tests/test_delivery.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py

- cd docs && npm run build

commit.message:
feat(guardian): complete Batch D learning quality pass

Summary:
- add comparable guardian evidence, weighted support, and policy-time arbitration across live and procedural learning
- scope procedural guidance by thread/project without broader-tier mixing and ground delivery lessons to explicit transport evidence
- record the Batch D review trail and shipped-state updates in implementation docs

Rationale:
- Batch D closes the overlay-order gap in guardian learning and makes durable procedural memory usable at policy time without stale or incomparable evidence
- unsupported phrasing/cadence/thread inference was fabricating durable lessons, so those axes now stay neutral until runtime records explicit variants

Tests:

commit.message:
fix(memory): isolate scoped procedural guidance bundles

Summary:
- select one winning procedural-guidance scope tier before resolving lessons so broader fallback lanes cannot leak extra biases into a narrower context
- require exact context-key matches on scoped memory reads and persist active_project on guardian interventions so runtime refreshes can write and reload thread/project guidance consistently
- add direct regressions for mixed-scope leakage and record the follow-up in the implementation log

Rationale:
- the first context-aware resolver still merged lessons per axis across thread+project, thread, project, and global scopes
- that let global negative cadence guidance leak into a scoped Atlas bundle and flip a real delivery decision back to bundle

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/memory/procedural_guidance.py backend/tests/test_guardian_feedback.py backend/tests/test_delivery.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py
- .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_prefers_thread_and_project_scope tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_prefers_thread_scope_over_project_scope tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_falls_back_from_thread_project_to_project_to_global tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_prefers_scoped_thread_and_project_context tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_does_not_mix_broader_lessons_into_selected_scope tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_feedback_refresh_writes_thread_and_project_scoped_memories tests/test_delivery.py::test_deliver_prefers_scoped_project_and_thread_guidance_over_global_memory tests/test_delivery.py::test_deliver_prefers_thread_scoped_procedural_guidance_before_project_or_global tests/test_delivery.py::test_deliver_prefers_context_scoped_guidance_over_conflicting_global_memory tests/test_guardian_state.py::test_build_guardian_state_prefers_scoped_project_guidance_over_global_memory tests/test_guardian_state.py::test_build_guardian_state_prefers_thread_scoped_procedural_guidance_before_project_fallback tests/test_guardian_state.py::test_build_guardian_state_prefers_context_scoped_guidance_over_global_memory -q
- cd docs && npm run build

COE-307: Normalize LiteLLM request data into canonical request tables (#20)

* feat(normalization): implement request normalizer job with reconciliation reporting

Summary:
- Create normalize_requests.py with RequestNormalizer and RequestNormalizerJob classes
- Implement ReconciliationReport for tracking unmapped rows with actionable diagnostics
- Add UnmappedRowDiagnostics for detailed per-row failure analysis
- Wire normalization job into CLI with `benchmark normalize run` and `benchmark normalize report` commands
- Export new classes from collectors package
- Add comprehensive unit tests (30 tests covering normalization, reporting, and job execution)

Rationale:
- LiteLLM request data needs normalization into canonical Request ORM entities
- Session correlation keys (experiment_id, variant_id, etc.) must be preserved in metadata
- Idempotent bulk insert via SQLRequestRepository prevents duplicates
- Reconciliation report surfaces unmapped rows with missing field counts and error categories
- CLI integration enables operator-driven normalization workflows

Implementation Details:
- RequestNormalizer handles timestamp parsing (ISO format, Unix epoch, multiple field names)
- Supports latency extraction (seconds->ms conversion, multiple field variants)
- Token counts extracted from nested usage object or top-level fields
- Error detection from error field or response.error
- Cache hit detection from cache_hit or cached fields
- Correlation keys searched in both top-level and nested metadata

Tests:
- All 30 new normalization tests pass
- All 210 existing tests pass
- Lint clean on all new files

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- Mark all acceptance criteria as complete

- Update test count (240 total, 30 new)

- Refactor dry-run mode to use RequestNormalizer directly (avoid type: ignore)

- Add verify_normalization.py script for runtime evidence

Rationale:
- PR review requested fixes for type safety, stub implementation, error categorization
- Evidence script demonstrates end-to-end normalization without requiring LiteLLM proxy

Evidence:
- PYTHONPATH=src python scripts/verify_normalization.py shows all demos passing
- 30/30 normalization tests passing
- Lint clean on all modified files

- Fix bulk insert failure handling: now raises RuntimeError instead of conflating with data quality issues

This separates database/repository failures (infrastructure) from data quality
issues (normalization), allowing proper error handling by the caller.

commit.message:
fix(demo): Address PR #10 feedback - fix demo_session_service.py runtime errors (#21)

* COE-305: Implement repository layer and benchmark metadata services

## Repository Layer (src/benchmark_core/repositories/)

New SQLAlchemy-based repositories for all canonical entities:
- base.py: AbstractRepository, SQLAlchemyRepository, RepositoryError hierarchy
- provider_repository.py: SQLProviderRepository with model management
- variant_repository.py: SQLVariantRepository with filtering by provider/harness
- experiment_repository.py: SQLExperimentRepository with variant associations
- task_card_repository.py: SQLTaskCardRepository with search capabilities
- harness_profile_repository.py: SQLHarnessProfileRepository
- session_repository.py: SQLSessionRepository with safe session creation/finalization
- request_repository.py: SQLRequestRepository with idempotent batch operations

## Service Layer (src/benchmark_core/services/)

New services for benchmark metadata management:
- session_service.py: Safe session lifecycle (create/finalize) with validation
- provider_service.py: Provider configuration management with model aliases
- variant_service.py: Variant definition and benchmark tags
- experiment_service.py: Experiment grouping and variant associations
- task_card_service.py: Task definition management
- harness_profile_service.py: Harness configuration with env rendering
- credential_service.py: Session-scoped proxy credential issuance
- benchmark_metadata_service.py: Unified service for cross-entity operations

## Key Features

- Referential integrity preserved via explicit FK checks and error handling
- Duplicate identifier rejection with DuplicateIdentifierError
- Safe session lifecycle: create with validation, finalize atomically
- Idempotent batch request ingestion for collectors
- Full test coverage: 52 new tests, all passing (129 total)

Renamed existing files for backward compatibility:
- repositories.py -> repositories_abc.py
- services.py -> services_abc.py

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* COE-305: Fix linting errors in test files - rename SessionLocal to session_local

* COE-305: Fix linting errors in test_db.py - rename SessionLocal to session_local

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* COE-305: Address PR review feedback - fix SQL injection and dead code

- Escape SQL LIKE wildcards in task_card_repository.py search_by_goal to prevent injection
- Fix dead code in request_repository.py create_many - move flush outside loop with proper rollback handling

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>

* COE-305: Add runtime evidence demo script and evidence output

- Add demo_session_service.py to demonstrate acceptance criteria
- Add EVIDENCE_COE305.md with runtime verification output
- All 3 acceptance criteria verified via live demonstration

* COE-305: Fix mypy type errors in repository layer - add CursorResult casting

* COE-305: Fix merge conflicts and repository compatibility issues

- Merge origin/main into COE-305 branch
- Fix SQLSessionRepository.finalize_session() to accept ended_at parameter
- Fix UUID handling in create_session_safe for SQLAlchemy compatibility
- Update CLI to use SQLSessionRepository consistently
- Fix test_services to use SQLSessionRepository in fixture
- Fix session.id references to session.session_id in tests
- All 180 tests now pass

* COE-305: Fix CollectionJobService to use create_many instead of create_batch

* fix(demo): correct demo_session_service.py runtime errors

Summary:
- Wrap SQLAlchemy Session in SQLSessionRepository before passing to SessionService
- Use session.session_id instead of session.id (domain model uses session_id field)
- Add timezone-aware/naive datetime handling for SQLite compatibility
- Import SQLSessionRepository in demo script

Rationale:
The demo script was crashing with AttributeError because SessionService expects
a SQLSessionRepository but received a raw SQLAlchemy Session. Additionally, the
Session domain model uses 'session_id' field (not 'id'), causing AttributeError
when accessing session.id. The datetime comparison also failed due to SQLite
storing naive timestamps while Python datetime.now(UTC) creates aware timestamps.

These fixes address outstanding critical feedback from PR #10 that was merged
before all runtime issues were resolved.

Tests:
- Ran scripts/demo_session_service.py - all 3 demos pass
- Ran pytest tests/unit/test_services.py - 32 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(services): remove duplicate imports in session_service.py

---------

Co-authored-by: openhands <openhands@all-hands.dev>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(doctor): Remove automatic rehydration; add explicit CLI rehydration command (#61)

* Simplify conversation resumption by removing brittle rehydration logic

This change removes the complex LLM configuration drift detection and
conversation rehydration logic that was causing issues on every orchestrator
restart. Instead, we now adopt a simpler "dumb" approach that just reuses
conversations as-is, similar to how the OpenHands CLI behaves.

Key changes:

1. Removed LLM config fingerprint comparison and drift detection
   - LlmConfigFingerprint now only tracks model name for observability
   - Removed api_key_hash and base_url_hash fields
   - Removed sha2 hashing dependency

2. Removed rehydration methods:
   - rehydrate_session_for_llm_config_change
   - rehydrate_existing_session
   - build_rehydration_context

3. Removed conversation summary generation before rehydration:
   - Removed extract_message_history
   - Removed build_summarization_prompt
   - Removed generate_conversation_summary
   - Removed preserved_conversation_summary field from manifest

4. Removed token preservation logic for rehydration

5. Simplified try_resume_existing_session to just attach directly:
   - No more checking current vs stored LLM config
   - No more deleting and recreating conversations
   - Just attach to existing conversation_id and use stored config

6. Updated test expectations:
   - Renamed test to issue_session_runner_reuses_conversation_despite_llm_config_changes
   - Now expects continuation prompt instead of full prompt
   - Expects conversation reuse (not fresh) despite config changes
   - Verifies original API key is preserved in stored config

7. Updated documentation across 4 files to reflect simplified behavior

The previous rehydration logic was brittle because it would detect false
positives on every orchestrator restart due to subtle differences in how
LLM configs were resolved. This caused conversations to be unnecessarily
deleted and recreated, losing conversation history and requiring complex
recovery logic with Linear workpad context.

The new approach trusts that the stored conversation configuration in
meta.json is valid and simply reuses the conversation as-is. This is more
reliable and matches user expectations from the OpenHands CLI experience.

* Add explicit, decoupled conversation rehydration capability

This commit adds explicit rehydration functionality that is decoupled from the
normal conversation resumption flow. Rehydration is NOT automatically triggered
- it must be explicitly called when truly needed (e.g., corruption, export/import).

Changes:

1. RehydrationResult struct
   - Contains the new session, context for the prompt, and old conversation ID
   - Provides a clean API for accessing rehydration results

2. RehydrationOptions struct
   - Configurable options for rehydration (reason, summarization, max events)
   - Default: summarize=true, max_summary_events=50

3. IssueSessionRunner::rehydrate_conversation() method
   - Explicitly callable method to rehydrate a conversation
   - Creates fresh session with current configuration
   - Optionally summarizes old conversation history
   - Preserves token metrics through the manifest (already worked)

4. build_rehydration_context()
   - Builds context summarizing the old conversation history
   - Includes conversation metadata (ID, model, status)
   - Provides structured context for the new conversation

5. summarize_conversation()
   - Extracts key events from old conversation (messages, observations, errors)
   - Configurable maximum events to include
   - Formats as readable summary for context

6. UnexpectedEarlyResult error variant
   - New error type for when rehydration returns early result unexpectedly

7. lib.rs exports
   - Exported RehydrationResult and RehydrationOptions for public API

Design Decisions:
- Rehydration is opt-in, not automatic - avoids false positives on orchestrator restart
- Normal flow simply reuses conversations as-is (the "dumb" approach)
- Rehydration available for specific scenarios: corruption, export/import, etc.
- When rehydrating, optionally summarizes old conversation and provides context
- Token metrics preserved through manifest (no special handling needed)

This addresses the root cause where automatic rehydration was causing issues
on every orchestrator restart by making rehydration an explicit, intentional
operation rather than an automatic side effect of config comparison.

* fix(doctor): Make --rehydrate recreate all conversations with current API key

The doctor --rehydrate command was only rehydrating conversations that failed
to attach (404 or other errors). However, conversations that attached
successfully but had invalid/stale API keys were skipped. This left many
conversations with broken auth that would fail on first LLM call.

Changes:
- Doctor --rehydrate now unconditionally rehydrates ALL conversations
- Added API key fingerprint tracking to detect key changes in future
- Fixed token count preservation during rehydration
- Fixed apply_runtime_snapshot to not overwrite preserved token counts
- Fixed supervisor lifecycle to persist through rehydration

This ensures that when OH_SECRET_KEY or LLM_API_KEY changes, running
`opensymphony doctor --rehydrate` will fix all conversations by recreating
them with the current API key from environment

* fix(tests): Update test to expect reset when API key changes

* style: cargo fmt

* style: fix clippy warnings in TUI (manual_clamp, let_and_return)

* fix(doctor): Rehydrate all conversations unconditionally with current API key

- Fixed apply_runtime_snapshot() to conditionally update token counts
  (only if stream has higher values, preventing reset to 0)
- Fixed accumulate_tokens() to properly read accumulated totals from conversation state
- Added API key fingerprint tracking for detecting LLM config drift
- Updated test to expect conversation reset on API key change
- Fixed collapsible_if clippy warning in test

* style: cargo fmt

* fix: resolve clippy warnings blocking CI

Summary:
- Prefix unused conversation_id variable with underscore in test
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] to unused test helpers
- Simplify redundant closures in workspace config builders
- Move helper functions and imports before test module to fix
  items-after-test-module lint

Rationale:
Clippy was failing with -D warnings on:
- unused_variables in issue_session_runner test
- dead_code for StaticWorkpadCommentSource and workpad_comment helpers
- redundant_closure in four HookDefinition::shell calls
- items_after_test_module for run_rehydrate_command and helpers

Tests:
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (passes)
- cargo test --workspace (all 250+ tests pass)
- cargo fmt --check (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: delete old conversation on API key drift to prevent resource leak

When API key fingerprint differs between stored manifest and current
environment, delete the old conversation before returning Reset. This
prevents orphaned conversations when API keys rotate.

* fix: remove API key fingerprint drift detection

Simplified conversation resumption: no longer detect or handle API key
changes automatically. Conversations are reused as-is with their stored
config. If API key is actually invalid, attach will fail naturally and
explicit rehydration via CLI can be used.

- Removed fingerprint comparison logic from try_reuse_session()
- Updated test to expect conversation reuse despite API key changes

* docs: add rehydration documentation

- Added rehydration section to testing-and-operations.md with:
  - When to use rehydration
  - Command examples (single issue, bulk, with options)
  - How rehydration works
  - Difference between normal resumption and rehydration
- Added rehydrate commands to README.md quick start

* style: cargo fmt

* test: add rehydrate_conversation test coverage per PR review

Add comprehensive test `rehydrate_conversation_deletes_old_and_creates_new_with_token_preservation` that verifies:
- Old conversation is deleted from server (404 verified)
- New conversation is created with different ID
- Token counts (input/output/cache) are preserved across rehydration

Also refactors run_doctor_rehydration() return type from Vec<RehydrationAttemptResult>
to (usize, usize) tuple for cleaner success/failure reporting.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(changelog): record startup preop quiet fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased startup PREOP provisioning changelog
  placeholder with the short hash for the shipped fix commit

Rationale:
- this repo requires release-note-worthy unreleased entries to carry
  the concrete fix hash before handoff

Validation:
- git show --stat --oneline --no-patch 1e10075

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(master): keep startup preop provisioning quiet

Summary:
- keep `configure_slave/2` in startup-held `:preop_ready` sessions from
  pushing live health polling into slaves that still remain targeted at
  `:preop`
- add focused master FSM coverage for the actual slave configure payload
  used by the PREOP config path
- add simulator scenario 51 and align the simulator README and
  changelog with the reproduced regression

Rationale:
- a plain PREOP reconfigure was enough to arm background polling and
  turn a configuration-only session into `:recovering` on disconnect
- the master still needs to store the requested config for later use,
  but the live slave runtime should stay on the startup-suppressed
  watchdog until activation begins
- keep the reproducer in history with the triggering scenario:
  test/integration/simulator/51_preop_reconfigure_keeps_startup_poll_suppressed_test.exs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/51_preop_reconfigure_keeps_startup_poll_suppressed_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/49_preop_provisioning_activation_restores_health_poll_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/50_preop_provisioning_held_preop_health_poll_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(changelog): record held preop polling fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased mixed PREOP provisioning changelog
  placeholder with the short hash for the shipped fix commit

Rationale:
- release-note-worthy unreleased entries in this repo must carry the
  concrete fix hash before handoff

Validation:
- git show --stat --oneline --no-patch d126047

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(runtime): restore held preop provisioning polling

Summary:
- restore configured `health_poll_ms` for slaves intentionally left in
  `:preop` when mixed PREOP-first provisioning activation starts an
  operational session
- re-arm or cancel the PREOP health poll timeout immediately after a
  live PREOP reconfigure succeeds instead of waiting for a later state
  transition
- add simulator scenario 50 plus focused activation and slave-FSM
  coverage for the reproduced regression

Rationale:
- PREOP-first mixed activation left held PREOP slaves on the startup
  watchdog suppression path, so later disconnects stayed invisible
- pushing the config back into the slave runtime was not enough on its
  own because a successful PREOP reconfigure did not reschedule the
  timeout action
- keep the reproducer in history with the triggering scenario:
  test/integration/simulator/50_preop_provisioning_held_preop_health_poll_test.exs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_activation_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/50_preop_provisioning_held_preop_health_poll_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(changelog): record preop provisioning fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased PREOP-first provisioning changelog placeholder
  with the short hash for the shipped fix commit

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy unreleased entries to carry the
  concrete fix hash before handoff

Validation:
- git show --stat --oneline --no-patch 8521af7

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(master): restore preop provisioning activation target

Summary:
- treat target-state-only provisioning updates as local PREOP
  reconfigure work so startup-suppressed slave health polling is
  restored before activation
- restore `desired_runtime_target: :op` when `activate/0` runs from
  `:preop_ready`, and cover that path with focused unit tests
- add simulator scenario 49 and align the simulator README and
  changelog with the captured regression

Rationale:
- PREOP-first provisioning should regain normal runtime watchdog
  behavior once a discovered slave is configured for OP
- activation from `:preop_ready` must converge later recovery back to
  `:operational`, not the startup PREOP target
- keeping the numbered scenario and note in-tree ties the fix to its
  reproducer: test/integration/simulator/49_preop_provisioning_activation_restores_health_poll_test.exs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
  test/ethercat/master_activation_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test
  test/integration/simulator/49_preop_provisioning_activation_restores_health_poll_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(changelog): record held preop fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased mixed target-state PREOP changelog placeholder with
  the real fix commit hash

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy unreleased entries to point at the
  actual change hash before handoff
- this keeps the tested simulator fix commit intact while making the changelog
  entry concrete

Validation:
- git status --short

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(master): preserve held preop slave targets

Summary:
- add simulator scenario 48 for an operational ring with a mailbox slave
  intentionally held in :preop
- narrow startup PREOP health-poll suppression to configuration-only sessions
  where the whole runtime target is :preop
- recover runtime slave faults to the effective per-slave target so held
  PREOP slaves reconnect back to :preop instead of being promoted to :op
- add master unit coverage for the held-PREOP reconnect decision

Rationale:
- mixed target-state sessions still need runtime fault visibility on held
  service slaves even when PDO domains stay healthy
- reconnect retries must preserve the configured target for non-activatable
  slaves instead of blindly following the session-wide :op target
- Scenario: test/integration/simulator/
  48_operational_held_preop_slave_reconnect_test.exs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/48_operational_held_preop_slave_reconnect_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(changelog): record startup preop fix hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased startup PREOP health-poll changelog placeholder
  with the real fix commit hash

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy unreleased entries to point at the
  actual change hash before handoff
- this keeps the scenario fix commit intact while making the changelog entry
  concrete

Validation:
- git status --short

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(startup): disable startup preop health polling

Summary:
- stop passing configured health polling into slave startup when a slave is
  intentionally targeted to :preop
- add simulator scenario 47 documenting and reproducing the startup-held
  :preop regression
- register the scenario in the simulator loop and add an unreleased
  changelog entry

Rationale:
- configuration-only sessions that intentionally stop in :preop should not
  behave like active runtime watchdog sessions
- runtime-held :preop and :safeop health polling stays unchanged because the
  fix only changes the startup boundary
- Scenario: test/integration/simulator/
  47_startup_preop_health_poll_isolation_test.exs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/integration/simulator/47_startup_preop_health_poll_isolation_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/slave_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/master_test.exs test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/46_latched_al_error_safeop_retreat_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/05_safeop_retreat_with_health_poll_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator

feat: scaffold Roast Roulette — AI that roasts your online presence

Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects + ElevenLabs (TTS, SFX, Music)

- RoastSessionAgent DO: URL scraping, Workers AI roast script gen, ElevenLabs TTS + SFX delivery
- LeaderboardAgent DO: real-time top roasts
- React 19 + Vite + Tailwind frontend
- Animated hype score, live transcript, share to X
- SFX cache in R2 for instant reaction sounds

Built for ElevenHacks #2 (Cloudflare x ElevenLabs hackathon)
